truthSpec.title: Rate Limiter
truthSpec.overview: Implement a rate limiter that uses a sliding window algorithm with per-user AND per-endpoint tracking, backed by a Redis-compatible store interface.

requirements.text: Implement sliding window rate limiting (not fixed window)
Track limits per-user AND per-endpoint (composite key: userId + endpoint)
Default limit: 100 requests per window per user-endpoint pair
Return an object with: allowed (boolean), remaining (number), retryAfterMs (number | null)
Accept a RedisStore interface for storage (get, set, del methods)

edgeCases: First request from a user-endpoint pair is always allowed
Requests at exact window boundary should start a new window
Different endpoints for the same user should have independent limits
truthSpec.testSummary: 5 tests covering: basic allow/deny, sliding window behavior, per-user-endpoint independence, remaining count accuracy, and retry-after timing.

aiBrief.title: Rate Limiter
aiBrief.overview: Implement a rate limiter that tracks request counts and limits users when they exceed their quota.
requirements: Implement fixed window rate limiting (reset counter at window boundary)
Track limits per user using a Map<string, number> for storage
Default limit: 100 requests per window per user
Return an object with: allowed (boolean), remaining (number)

flaws.description: Brief says fixed window; truth requires sliding window algorithm
Brief says in-memory Map; truth requires RedisStore interface. Also omits per-endpoint tracking and retryAfterMs field.
